---
title: "Gemini API Reference"
---

## Model

NanoMeme uses **Nano Banana 2** (`gemini-2.0-flash-exp`) for image generation via the Gemini REST API.

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Model ID | `gemini-2.0-flash-exp` |
| Capability | Text-to-image generation |
| Text rendering | Near-zero error rate |
| Response format | Base64 PNG in JSON |

## API Endpoint

```
POST https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/{modelId}:generateContent?key={apiKey}
```

## Request Structure

Both platforms send identical JSON payloads:

```json
{
  "contents": [
    {
      "parts": [
        { "text": "A photorealistic portrait of..." }
      ],
      "role": "user"
    }
  ],
  "generationConfig": {
    "responseMimeType": "image/png",
    "responseModalities": ["Text", "Image"]
  }
}
```

### Platform Implementation

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Swift (macOS)

```swift
struct GenerateContentRequest: Encodable {
    let contents: [Content]
    let generationConfig: GenerationConfig?
}

let request = GenerateContentRequest(
    contents: [Content(parts: [.text(prompt)], role: "user")],
    generationConfig: GenerationConfig(
        responseMimeType: "image/png",
        responseModalities: ["Text", "Image"]
    )
)
```

## TypeScript (Mobile)

```typescript
interface GenerateContentRequest {
  contents: Content[];
  generationConfig?: GenerationConfig;
}

function buildGenerateRequest(
  prompt: string,
  config?: Partial<GenerationConfig>,
): GenerateContentRequest {
  return {
    contents: [{ parts: [{ text: prompt }], role: "user" }],
    generationConfig: {
      responseMimeType: "image/png",
      responseModalities: ["Text", "Image"],
      ...config,
    },
  };
}
```

:::

## Response Structure

```json
{
  "candidates": [
    {
      "content": {
        "parts": [
          {
            "inlineData": {
              "mimeType": "image/png",
              "data": "<base64-encoded-image>"
            }
          }
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

The image is extracted by finding the first `InlineDataPart` in the response candidates.

## Error Handling

### PROHIBITED_CONTENT

The Gemini API rejects prompts referencing copyrighted characters. The error response includes:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "message": "Content generation blocked due to safety filters",
    "status": "PROHIBITED_CONTENT",
    "code": 400
  }
}
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Swift

```swift
struct GeminiAPIError: LocalizedError {
    let message: String
    let status: String
    let httpStatusCode: Int

    var isProhibitedContent: Bool {
        status == "PROHIBITED_CONTENT"
    }
}
```

## TypeScript

```typescript
class GeminiAPIError extends Error {
  constructor(
    message: string,
    public readonly status: string,
    public readonly httpStatusCode: number,
  ) { super(message); }

  get isProhibitedContent(): boolean {
    return this.status === "PROHIBITED_CONTENT";
  }
}
```

:::

### Copyright-Safe Yoda Prompt

To avoid PROHIBITED_CONTENT rejections, the Yoda prompt uses only physical traits without IP-specific names:

> A photorealistic portrait of a small, ancient humanoid alien elder, approximately 66 centimeters tall, with bright green deeply wrinkled skin. Large pointed ears extending sideways from the head...

The overlay text "Make or make not. There is no buy" is a play on the original quote, used for parody/commentary purposes.

## API Key Security

| Platform | Storage | Mechanism |
|----------|---------|-----------|
| macOS | Keychain | `Security.framework` with service ID |
| Mobile | SecureStore | `expo-secure-store` (iOS Keychain / Android Keystore) |
| Fallback | Environment | `GEMINI_API_KEY` env var (macOS only) |

Both platforms encrypt API keys at rest using hardware-backed security. Neither stores keys in plain text (no UserDefaults, no AsyncStorage).
